title: Warm Buttery Croatian Chicken Pilaf
description: A rich and creamy Croatian dish featuring chicken, creme fraiche, and a hint of spice, perfect for a date night. This recipe uses a pressure cooker to achieve a tender and flavorful pilaf.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of butter in the pressure cooker over medium heat until melted.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the chicken breast pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the creme fraiche, chicken broth, uncooked white rice, smoked paprika, and remaining 2 tablespoons of butter to the pressure cooker.
Close the lid and set the valve to sealing, then cook on high pressure for 10 minutes.
Let the pressure release naturally for 10 minutes, then quick release any remaining pressure.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
